titleStmt.title: Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.17

div.p: William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know us to have conceded, and by this our charter to have made firm, the amicable peace made between the convent and church of Kelso on the one side, and the convent and church of Melrose on the other side, over the boundaries of the lands of Bowden, Eildon, and Darnick. Wherefore we will and instruct that the aforesaid peace made between the aforesaid convents and houses be firmly and inviolably held in times to come and for ever, just as it is more plainly specified in the cyrograph made between the aforesaid convents and houses. We have also conceded and by this our charter made firm the amicable peace made between the aforesaid convents and aforesaid houses over the dispute that was at issue between the aforesaid convents and houses aforenamed, about the land of Mow and of Clifton, just as it is specified in the aforesaid cyrograph made between the two. As witnesses: Alexander the lord king's son; Philip de Valognes, chamberlain; Robert of London, the lord king's son; William Comyn, David of Lindsay, justices; Oliver the lord king's chaplain; William del Bois, the lord king's clerk; Philip of Mowbray; Walter Oliphant; Henry the son of Earl David; Philip of Lundin; David the marischal; Richard son of Hugh; Herbert de Camera; John of Stirling; Richard, Adam Hastings. At Stirling on the 22nd day of June.
